mnemonics: BREAK Your Data Journey: Break, Return, Exit, Abort, Kill - the ways to jump out of a data processing loop.
keyPoints: The 'break' statement terminates the nearest enclosing loop prematurely.
The 'continue' statement skips the rest of the current loop iteration and moves to the next one.
The 'pass' statement is a null operation placeholder that does nothing, used syntactically where code is required.
commonMistakes: Using 'break' outside of a loop causes a SyntaxError.
Confusing 'continue' (skips to next iteration) with 'break' (exits entire loop).
Using 'pass' unnecessarily when actual code logic is needed, creating silent errors.
